papers.title_en: VSearcher: Long-Horizon Multimodal Search Agent via Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Ruiyang Zhang, Qianguo Sun, Chao Song, Yiyan Qi, Zhedong Zheng
papers.evidence: 基于多模态大模型的搜索智能体
papers.tldr: 本研究提出VSearcher，旨在将静态多模态模型转化为具备长程、多轮工具使用能力的搜索智能体。针对现有模型缺乏实时网页信息获取能力的问题，通过强化学习（RL）使其掌握文本/图像搜索及网页浏览。研究引入了迭代注入数据合成流程生成高质量QA，并采用SFT加RL的训练方案。实验表明，VSearcher在多模态搜索基准测试中表现优异，甚至超越了部分闭源模型。
papers.abstract_en: Large models are increasingly becoming autonomous agents that interact with real-world environments and use external tools to augment their static capabilities. However, most recent progress has focused on text-only large language models, which are limited to a single modality and therefore have narrower application scenarios. On the other hand, multimodal large models, while offering stronger perceptual capabilities, remain limited to static knowledge and lack the ability to access and leverage up-to-date web information. In this paper, we propose VSearcher, turning static multimodal model into multimodal search agent capable of long-horizon, multi-turn tool use in real-world web environments, including text search, image search, and web browsing, via reinforcement learning. Specifically, we introduce Iterative Injection Data Synthesis pipeline to generate large-scale, complex multimodal QA questions, which are further filtered with comprehensive metrics to ensure high quality and sufficient difficulty. We then adopt an SFT-then-RL training pipeline to turn base multimodal models to agent capable of multi-turn tool calling in real-world web environments. Besides, we propose a multimodal search benchmark MM-SearchExam dedicated to evaluating search capabilities of multimodal search agents, which proves highly challenging for recent proprietary models. Extensive evaluations across multiple multimodal search benchmarks reveal effectiveness of our method. VSearcher achieves superior performance compared to recent multimodal search agents and even surpasses several proprietary models on multimodal web search tasks.

papers.title_en: MuxTune: Efficient Multi-Task LLM Fine-Tuning in Multi-Tenant Datacenters via Spatial-Temporal Backbone Multiplexing
papers.authors: Chunyu Xue, Yi Pan, Weihao Cui, Quan Chen, Shulai Zhang, Bingsheng He, Minyi Guo
papers.evidence: 高效的多任务 LLM 微调
papers.tldr: MuxTune 针对多租户数据中心中大模型参数高效微调（PEFT）存在的 GPU 利用率低和通信延迟导致的设备停顿问题，提出了一种时空骨干网络复用系统。通过统一 PEFT 表示和层次化协同调度，MuxTune 实现了多任务的并发执行，并结合混合并行策略与块状数据对齐技术，显著提升了微调吞吐量并降低了显存占用，为高效多任务微调提供了新方案。
papers.abstract_en: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) is widely applied as the backend of fine-tuning APIs for large language model (LLM) customization in datacenters. Service providers deploy separate instances for individual PEFT tasks, giving rise to prominent resource inefficiencies, including (1) GPU underutilization from small-scale, PEFT-native operators and (2) device stalls from communication delays and data dependencies in parallelized execution. To address these issues, this paper presents MuxTune, a fine-tuning system that enables resource-efficient concurrent execution of multiple PEFT tasks. The key idea is to multiplex the backbone across independent tasks in a spatial-temporal manner for improved utilization and reduced stalls. Building on flexible, modularized backbone sharing via unified PEFT representations, MuxTune proposes hierarchical co-scheduling scheme with task, operator, and data-level optimizations. Specifically, it fuses tasks through a hybrid of spatial and temporal multiplexing, and orchestrates multi-task operator execution in two-tiered hybrid parallelism. Additionally, MuxTune employs chunk-based data alignment to mitigate inter-task ineffective tokens. Experimental results demonstrate that MuxTune achieves up to $2.33\times$ higher throughput and $5.29\times$ memory reduction compared to three state-of-the-art baselines.

papers.title_en: ProGIC: Progressive and Lightweight Generative Image Compression with Residual Vector Quantization
papers.authors: Hao Cao, Chengbin Liang, Wenqi Guo, Zhijin Qin, Jungong Han
papers.evidence: 基于残差向量量化的轻量级生成式图像压缩
papers.tldr: 本研究针对生成式图像压缩（GIC）模型体积大、部署难及缺乏传输灵活性等问题，提出了ProGIC。该方法结合残差向量量化（RVQ）技术与轻量化骨干网络（深度可分离卷积与小注意力模块），实现了从粗糙到精细的渐进式比特流传输。实验表明，ProGIC在DISTS和LPIPS指标上较MS-ILLM节省了约58%的比特率，且编解码速度提升10倍以上，兼顾了高感知质量与极高的运行效率。
papers.abstract_en: Recent advances in generative image compression (GIC) have delivered remarkable improvements in perceptual quality. However, many GICs rely on large-scale and rigid models, which severely constrain their utility for flexible transmission and practical deployment in low-bitrate scenarios. To address these issues, we propose Progressive Generative Image Compression (ProGIC), a compact codec built on residual vector quantization (RVQ). In RVQ, a sequence of vector quantizers encodes the residuals stage by stage, each with its own codebook. The resulting codewords sum to a coarse-to-fine reconstruction and a progressive bitstream, enabling previews from partial data. We pair this with a lightweight backbone based on depthwise-separable convolutions and small attention blocks, enabling practical deployment on both GPUs and CPU-only devices. Experimental results show that ProGIC attains comparable compression performance compared with previous methods. It achieves bitrate savings of up to 57.57% on DISTS and 58.83% on LPIPS compared to MS-ILLM on the Kodak dataset. Beyond perceptual quality, ProGIC enables progressive transmission for flexibility, and also delivers over 10 times faster encoding and decoding compared with MS-ILLM on GPUs for efficiency.

papers.title_en: Beyond Language Modeling: An Exploration of Multimodal Pretraining
papers.authors: Shengbang Tong, David Fan, John Nguyen, Ellis Brown, Gaoyue Zhou, Shengyi Qian, Boyang Zheng, Théophane Vallaeys, Junlin Han, Rob Fergus, Naila Murray, Marjan Ghazvininejad, Mike Lewis, Nicolas Ballas, Amir Bar, Michael Rabbat, Jakob Verbeek, Luke Zettlemoyer, Koustuv Sinha, Yann LeCun, Saining Xie
papers.evidence: 原生多模态模型预训练
papers.tldr: 本研究旨在探索原生多模态基础模型的设计空间。通过从零开始的受控预训练实验，采用Transfusion框架（文本采用下文预测，视觉采用扩散模型），在文本、视频和图像等多样化数据上进行训练。研究发现：RAE是兼顾理解与生成的最佳视觉表示；视觉与语言数据具有互补协同效应；统一预训练能自然诱导世界建模能力；MoE架构能有效解决视觉比语言更耗数据的扩展不对称性，为构建高效多模态模型提供了路径。
papers.abstract_en: The visual world offers a critical axis for advancing foundation models beyond language. Despite growing interest in this direction, the design space for native multimodal models remains opaque. We provide empirical clarity through controlled, from-scratch pretraining experiments, isolating the factors that govern multimodal pretraining without interference from language pretraining. We adopt the Transfusion framework, using next-token prediction for language and diffusion for vision, to train on diverse data including text, video, image-text pairs, and even action-conditioned video. Our experiments yield four key insights: (i) Representation Autoencoder (RAE) provides an optimal unified visual representation by excelling at both visual understanding and generation; (ii) visual and language data are complementary and yield synergy for downstream capabilities; (iii) unified multimodal pretraining leads naturally to world modeling, with capabilities emerging from general training; and (iv) Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) enables efficient and effective multimodal scaling while naturally inducing modality specialization. Through IsoFLOP analysis, we compute scaling laws for both modalities and uncover a scaling asymmetry: vision is significantly more data-hungry than language. We demonstrate that the MoE architecture harmonizes this scaling asymmetry by providing the high model capacity required by language while accommodating the data-intensive nature of vision, paving the way for truly unified multimodal models.

papers.title_en: ByteFlow: Language Modeling through Adaptive Byte Compression without a Tokenizer
papers.authors: Chunyuan Deng, Sanket Lokegaonkar, Colin Lockard, Besnik Fetahu, Nasser Zalmout, Xian Li
papers.evidence: 语言模型的压缩驱动分词
papers.tldr: 针对现代语言模型依赖固定分词器导致的脆弱性，本文提出ByteFlow Net，一种无需分词器的层次化架构。该模型通过潜在表示的编码率驱动压缩，将原始字节流自适应地分割为语义单元，并利用Top-K选择保持静态计算图。实验证明，这种端到端的自适应分词策略在性能上优于传统的BPE Transformer和字节级模型，为构建更灵活、基于信息论的语言模型提供了新路径。
papers.abstract_en: Modern language models still rely on fixed, pre-defined subword tokenizations. Once a tokenizer is trained, the LM can only operate at this fixed level of granularity, which often leads to brittle and counterintuitive behaviors even in otherwise strong reasoning models. We introduce \textbf{ByteFlow Net}, a new hierarchical architecture that removes tokenizers entirely and instead enables models to learn their own segmentation of raw byte streams into semantically meaningful units. ByteFlow Net performs compression-driven segmentation based on the coding rate of latent representations, yielding adaptive boundaries \emph{while preserving a static computation graph via Top-$K$ selection}. Unlike prior self-tokenizing methods that depend on brittle heuristics with human-designed inductive biases, ByteFlow Net adapts its internal representation granularity to the input itself. Experiments demonstrate that this compression-based chunking strategy yields substantial performance gains, with ByteFlow Net outperforming both BPE-based Transformers and previous byte-level architectures. These results suggest that end-to-end, tokenizer-free modeling is not only feasible but also more effective, opening a path toward more adaptive and information-grounded language models.
